reference.title: GitHub Best Practices Reference
reference.p: This reference encapsulates key recommendations from GitHub’s official documentation for creating and managing repositories. Agents should use these guidelines as hard constraints when instantiating new repositories or maintaining existing ones.

section.title: README and community files
section.p: Every repository should include a `README.md` explaining the project’s purpose, how to install or build it, and how to contribute. GitHub notes that a README, along with a licence, contribution guidelines and a code of conduct, communicates expectations and helps manage contributions.

section.title: Security features
section.p: Public repositories should enable Dependabot alerts, secret scanning and push protection to detect vulnerabilities and prevent secrets from being pushed. A `SECURITY.md` file should describe how to report vulnerabilities and encourage responsible disclosure.

section.title: Code owners
section.p: Define individuals or teams responsible for different parts of the codebase using a `CODEOWNERS` file. People with write permissions can be listed as code owners, and repository administrators can require their approval before merging a pull request. Place the `CODEOWNERS` file in the `.github/`, root or `docs/` directory.

section.title: Branch protection
section.p: Create branch protection rules to prevent deletion or force‑pushing and to enforce requirements such as status checks, linear history and pull‑request reviews. Enabling branch protection helps ensure that important branches like `main` can only be updated through approved pull requests.

section.title: Issue templates and forms
section.p: Use issue templates and issue forms to collect structured data when contributors open issues. GitHub’s configuration guide explains how to create issue templates and forms in the `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE` folder and highlights that templates can set default titles, labels and assignees. Issue forms support input, dropdown, checkbox and file upload fields and are authored in YAML.

section.title: Pull request templates
section.p: Add a `pull_request_template.md` file in the repository root, `docs` or `.github` directory to automatically populate the body of new pull requests. The template should prompt authors to link related issues, describe the proposed changes and mention the reviewers.

section.title: Large files
section.p: When tracking large binaries or data files, use Git Large File Storage (LFS). GitHub recommends using Git LFS to avoid performance issues with large files.
